title: crepis chrysantha
intro: Crepis chrysantha, also known as the golden hawk's beard or golden ragwort, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat inflammation, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic. The plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, offering various health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Crepis chrysantha can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
